new_classes.definition_en: Spirituous beverages of CN subheading 2208 90 56, including aniseed spirits, raki, agave spirits other than tequila (e.g. mezcal), spirits distilled from aromatic herbs, digestive bitters, aquavit, kranawitter, spirits distilled from roots (e.g. gentian spirits), sorghum spirits, Hefebrand (lees spirit), and similar other spirits not elsewhere specified under heading 2208, presented in containers of 2 litres or less.
Distillation process using aniseed or anise-flavoured botanicals as the principal flavouring feedstock, yielding aniseed spirits such as pastis, ouzo, or raki.
Distillation process using agave plants (other than the blue agave used in tequila) as the principal fermentation and distillation feedstock, yielding spirits such as mezcal.

new_classes.label_en: herbal or root spirit distillation process
new_classes.definition_en: Distillation process using aromatic herbs, roots (e.g. gentian), or similar botanical materials as the principal flavouring feedstock, yielding spirits such as digestive bitters, aquavit, kranawitter, or gentian spirits.

new_classes.label_en: sorghum or lees (yeast lees) distillation feedstock
new_classes.definition_en: Raw material for distillation consisting of sorghum grain or wine/fermentation lees (Hefe/Trub), used to produce sorghum spirits or Hefebrand (lees spirit).

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms cover the main distinguishing criteria for CN 2208 90 56: (1) the product is a spirituous beverage of heading 2208 classified as 'other' (not tequila, not gin, not vodka, not rum, not whisky, not arrack, not liqueur, not brandy from grapes/plums/pears/cherries); (2) it is presented in containers ≤ 2 L (via someValuesFrom SmallContainer); (3) specific production processes are introduced for the main product types (aniseed spirits/raki, agave spirits other than tequila/mezcal, herbal/root spirits/digestive bitters/aquavit/kranawitter); (4) a sorghum-or-lees feedstock class covers sorghum spirits and Hefebrand. However, full coverage is limited because: (a) the 'other' catch-all nature of this subheading cannot be fully formalised as a necessary and sufficient condition without enumerating all excluded types as complements (not done to avoid over-commitment); (b) the restriction on hasDistillationProcess uses someValuesFrom (disjunctive, not a union), so it does not express that any of these processes is sufficient; (c) the complement restriction on AgaveDistillationProcess vs AniseedDistillationProcess was added only as an illustrative disjointness sample and does not capture all mutual exclusions; (d) no OWL union class is formed over all permitted distillation processes. Coverage is estimated at ~0.72.
